Create a Blazor Web App for a university course catalog.

Requirements:
- Browse departments and courses in a searchable, filterable list
- View course detail pages (description, schedule, credits, instructor bio)
- Students submit an enrollment request form (name, student ID, course selection) — standard HTML form, no wizards or real-time validation
- Homepage shows featured courses and department links
- No authentication — open to all students and visitors
- Hosted on the university intranet with fast, reliable connectivity

+
+        if (departmentId.HasValue && departmentId.Value > 0)
+            query = query.Where(c => c.DepartmentId == departmentId.Value);

+        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(searchTerm))

+            var term = searchTerm.Trim().ToLowerInvariant();

+        new() { Id = 12, DepartmentId = 5, CourseCode = "HIST 101", Title = "World History to 1500", Description = "Survey of major civilizations and events from ancient times through the late medieval period.", Credits = 3, Schedule = "Tue/Thu 11:00 AM–12:20 PM", InstructorName = "Prof. Andrew Smith", InstructorBio = "Prof. Smith is a medievalist who has conducted archaeological research across Europe.", IsFeatured = true },
+        new() { Id = 13, DepartmentId = 5, CourseCode = "HIST 201", Title = "Modern World History", Description = "From the Renaissance through the 21st century, focusing on political, social, and economic transformations.", Credits = 3, Schedule = "Mon/Wed 3:00–4:20 PM", InstructorName = "Dr. Linda Okafor", InstructorBio = "Dr. Okafor's research centers on post-colonial history and global migration patterns." },
+    ];
+
+    public List<Department> GetAllDepartments() => Departments;
+
+    public Department? GetDepartment(int id) => Departments.FirstOrDefault(d => d.Id == id);
+
+    public List<Course> GetAllCourses() => Courses;
+
+    public List<Course> GetFeaturedCourses() => Courses.Where(c => c.IsFeatured).ToList();
+
+    public List<Course> GetCoursesByDepartment(int departmentId) =>
+        Courses.Where(c => c.DepartmentId == departmentId).ToList();
+
+    public Course? GetCourse(int id) => Courses.FirstOrDefault(c => c.Id == id);
+
+    public List<Course> SearchCourses(string? searchTerm, int? departmentId)
+    {
+        var query = Courses.AsEnumerable();
+
+        if (departmentId.HasValue && departmentId.Value > 0)
+            query = query.Where(c => c.DepartmentId == departmentId.Value);
+
+        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(searchTerm))
+        {
+            var term = searchTerm.Trim().ToLowerInvariant();
+            query = query.Where(c =>
+                c.Title.Contains(term, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
+                c.CourseCode.Contains(term, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
+                c.InstructorName.Contains(term, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
+                c.Description.Contains(term, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
+        }
+
+        return query.ToList();
+    }
+}

data.content:
The Blazor Web App is complete. Here's what was created:

**Project:** `UniversityCourseCatalog` — a .NET Blazor Web App using static SSR (ideal for intranet with fast connectivity, no WebSocket overhead needed).

**Pages:**
- **Home** (`/`) — Featured courses cards + department links
- **Courses** (`/courses`) — Searchable, filterable table with text search and department dropdown
- **Course Detail** (`/courses/{id}`) — Full description, schedule, credits, and instructor bio
- **Enroll** (`/enroll`) — Standard HTML form with name, student ID, course selection, and server-side validation

**Architecture:**
- `Models/` — `Department`, `Course`, `EnrollmentRequest` (with data annotations)
- `Services/CatalogService` — In-memory catalog with 5 departments and 13 courses
- No authentication, no interactivity mode — pure server-rendered HTML forms
- Bootstrap styling from the default template

Run with: `cd UniversityCourseCatalog && dotnet run`
